text: Developing a mobile app involves several key decisions. First, you must choose between native development—building separate apps for iOS and Android using platform-specific languages like Swift or Kotlin—and cross-platform solutions like React Native or Flutter, which allow sharing code between systems. A critical aspect is responsive design: your app’s interface should adapt seamlessly to different screen sizes and orientations. Once built, you submit the app to official app stores (Google Play Store and Apple App Store), which review it for quality and security. Finally, consider performance: native apps often run faster and use device features more efficiently, while cross-platform apps can be quicker to develop but may require extra optimization to avoid lag.
